etymology.description: semantic 田(田) (tián, "field") + phonetic 介(介) (jiè) -> boundary; realm
etymology.interpretation: The character 界 (jiè, "boundary; realm") combines 田(田) (tián, "field") as the semantic component and 介(介) (jiè) as the phonetic component. Fields often have boundaries, and this association naturally leads to the concept of 'boundary' which is essential in defining realms or territories.

examples.chinese: 科学界不断地探索新领域(科學界不斷地探索新領域)。
examples.pinyin: Kēxuéjiè bùduàn dì tànsuǒ xīn lǐngyù.
examples.english: The scientific community continuously explores new fields.

examples.chinese: 艺术界有许多有才华的人(藝術界有許多有才華的人)。
examples.pinyin: Yìshùjiè yǒu xǔduō yǒu cáihuá de rén.
examples.english: There are many talented people in the art world.

examples.chinese: 电脑的世界中(電腦的世界中)，科技发展迅速(科技發展迅速)。
examples.pinyin: Diànnǎo de shìjiè zhōng, kējì fāzhǎn xùnsù.
examples.english: In the world of computers, technology develops rapidly.
